  # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n102\t    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n103\t    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n104\t    router_ms: int | None = None\n105\t\n106\t\n107\t# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n108\t# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n109\t# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\n110\tROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n111\t\n112\t# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n113\t_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n114\t    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n115\t    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n116\t    \"intent objects.\"\n117\t)\n118\t\n119\t\n120\tclass IntentRouter:\n121\t    \"\"\"\n122\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n123\t\n124\t    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n125\t    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n126\t    \"\"\"\n127\t\n128\t    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n129\t        self.store = store\n130\t        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n131\t        self._zai_client = None\n132\t        self._reload_manager = None\n133\t\n134\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n135\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n136\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n137\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n138\t        return self._zai_client\n139\t\n140\t    async def _get_store(self):\n141\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n142\t        if self.store is None:\n143\t            self.store = get_store()\n144\t        return self.store\n145\t\n146\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n147\t        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n148\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n149\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n150\t        return self._reload_manager\n151\t\n152\t    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n153\t        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n154\t        try:\n155\t            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n156\t        except Exception as e:\n157\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n158\t            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n159\t\n160\t    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n161\t        \"\"\"\n162\t        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n163\t\n164\t        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n165\t        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n166\t        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n167\t        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n168\t        \"\"\"\n169\t        try:\n170\t            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n171\t        except Exception as e:\n172\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n173\t            return \"\"\n174\t\n175\t    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n176\t        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n177\t        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n178\t        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n179\t        if urgency_prompt:\n180\t            system_prompt = (\n181\t                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n182\t            )\n183\t        return system_prompt\n184\t\n185\t    async def classify_utterance(\n186\t        self,\n187\t        utterance: str,\n188\t        session_id: str,\n189\t        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n190\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n191\t        \"\"\"\n192\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n193\t\n194\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n195\t\n196\t        Args:\n197\t            utterance: The user utterance\n198\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n199\t            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n200\t\n201\t        Returns:\n202\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n203\t\n204\t        Raises:\n205\t            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n206\t            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n207\t            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n208\t            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n209\t        \"\"\"\n210\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n211\t\n212\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n213\t        store = await self._get_store()\n214\t        session_context = \"\"\n215\t        try:\n216\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n217\t            if session:\n218\t                # Get recent intents for context\n219\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n220\t                if recent_intents:\n221\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n222\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n223\t                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n224\t        except Exception as e:\n225\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n226\t\n227\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n228\t\n229\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n230\t\n231\t        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n232\t        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n233\t        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n234\t\n235\t        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n236\t        retry_count = 0\n237\t        raw_response = None\n238\t\n239\t        try:\n240\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n241\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n242\t                user_message=user_message,\n243\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n244\t                max_tokens=2048,\n245\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n246\t            )\n247\t\n248\t            # Store raw response for error reporting\n249\t            raw_response = response\n250\t\n251\t            # Parse JSON response using centralized parser (optimized with manual fence stripping)\n252\t            # Let ParseLLMError bubble to retry handler below\n253\t            intents_data = parse_llm_response(response)\n254\t            classifications = []\n255\t\n256\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n257\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n258\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n259\t                try:\n260\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n261\t                except ValueError:\n262\t                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n263\t                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n264\t\n265\t                classification = IntentClassification(\n266\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n267\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n268\t                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n269\t                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n270\t                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n271\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n272\t                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n273\t                )\n274\t                classifications.append(classification)\n275\t\n276\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n277\t            return classifications\n278\t\n279\t        except ParseLLMError as e:\n280\t            \"\"\"\n281\t            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n282\t\n283\t            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n284\t            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n285\t            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n286\t            \"\"\"\n287\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n288\t            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n289\t\n290\t            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n291\t            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n292\t                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n293\t                retry_count += 1\n294\t                try:\n295\t                    # Retry once with same parameters\n296\t                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n297\t                        utterance=utterance,\n298\t                        session_id=session_id,\n299\t                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n300\t                    )\n301\t                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n302\t                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n303\t                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n304\t                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n305\t                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n306\t                        retry_count=retry_count,\n307\t                    ) from retry_e\n308\t\n309\t            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n310\t            raise RouterMalformedError(\n311\t                parse_error=str(e),\n312\t                raw_output=raw_output,\n313\t                retry_count=retry_count,\n314\t            ) from e\n315\t\n316\t        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n317\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n318\t            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n319\t\n320\t        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n321\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n322\t            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n323\t\n324\t        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n325\t            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n326\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n327\t\n328\t        except Exception as e:\n329\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n330\t            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n331\t            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n332\t\n333\t    async def route_utterance(\n334\t        self,\n335\t        utterance: str,\n336\t        utterance_id: str,\n337\t        session_id: str,\n338\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n339\t        \"\"\"\n340\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n341\t\n342\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n343\t\n344\t        Args:\n345\t            utterance: The user utterance\n346\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n347\t            session_id: Session ID\n348\t\n349\t        Returns:\n350\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n351\t\n352\t        Raises:\n353\t            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n354\t        \"\"\"\n355\t        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n356\t        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n357\t\n358\t        try:\n359\t            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n360\t            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n361\t            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n362\t            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n363\t            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n364\t            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n365\t\n366\t            routed_intents = []\n367\t            for classification in classifications:\n368\t                # Create intent ID\n369\t                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n370\t\n371\t                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n372\t                    intent_id=intent_id,\n373\t                    classification=classification,\n374\t                    session_id=session_id,\n375\t                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n376\t                    router_ms=router_ms,\n377\t                )\n378\t                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n379\t\n380\t            return routed_intents\n381\t\n382\t        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n383\t            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n384\t            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n385\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n386\t            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n387\t                utterance=utterance,\n388\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n389\t                session_id=session_id,\n390\t                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n391\t                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n392\t                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n393\t            )\n394\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n395\t\n396\t        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n397\t            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n398\t            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n399\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n400\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n401\t                utterance=utterance,\n402\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n403\t                session_id=session_id,\n404\t                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n405\t            )\n406\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n407\t\n408\t        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n409\t            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n410\t            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n411\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n412\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n413\t                utterance=utterance,\n414\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n415\t                session_id=session_id,\n416\t                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n417\t            )\n418\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n419\t\n420\t        except RouterProxyError as e:\n421\t            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n422\t            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n423\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n424\t            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n425\t                utterance=utterance,\n426\t                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n427\t                session_id=session_id,\n428\t                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n429\t            )\n430\t            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n431\t\n432\t    async def process_intent(\n433\t        self,\n434\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n435\t    ) -> dict:\n436\t        \"\"\"\n437\t        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n438\t\n439\t        Args:\n440\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n441\t\n442\t        Returns:\n443\t            Result dictionary with status and data\n444\t        \"\"\"\n445\t        classification = routed_intent.classification\n446\t\n447\t        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n448\t        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n449\t        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n450\t        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n451\t        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n452\t        # the dispatch itself.\n453\t        timings = DispatchTimings()\n454\t        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n455\t\n456\t        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n457\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n458\t            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n459\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n460\t            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n461\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n462\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n463\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n464\t                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n465\t            )\n466\t\n467\t            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n468\t            store = await self._get_store()\n469\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n470\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n471\t                \"resolved\",\n472\t                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n473\t            )\n474\t\n475\t            return {\n476\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n477\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n478\t                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n479\t                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n480\t            }\n481\t\n482\t        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n483\t            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n484\t            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n485\t            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n486\t                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n487\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n488\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n489\t            )\n490\t\n491\t            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n492\t            store = await self._get_store()\n493\t            await store.update_intent_status(\n494\t                routed_intent.intent_id,\n495\t                \"resolved\",\n496\t                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n497\t            )\n498\t\n499\t            return {\n500\t                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n501\t                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n502\t                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n503\t                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n504\t            }\n505\t\n506\t        try:\n507\t            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n508\t            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n509\t                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n510\t            else:\n511\t                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n512\t                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n513\t        except Exception:\n514\t            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n515\t            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n516\t            raise\n517\t\n518\t        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n519\t        return result\n520\t\n521\t    async def _persist_timings(\n522\t        self,\n523\t        intent_id: str,\n524\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n525\t    ) -> None:\n526\t        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n527\t        try:\n528\t            store = await self._get_store()\n529\t            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n530\t        except Exception as e:\n531\t            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n532\t\n533\t    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n534\t        self,\n535\t Args:\n542\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n543\t            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n544\t                fetch_total_ms, and routed_intent.classification\n550\t\n551\t        logger.info(\n552\t            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n553\t            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n554\t        )\n555\t\n556\t        try:\n557\t            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n558\t            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n559\t                classification.intent_type,\n560\t                classification.lookup_kind\n561\t            )\n562\t\n563\t            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n564\t            from ..registry import get_project\n565\t            repo_path = None\n566\t            ssh_target = None\n567\t            host_alias = None\n568\t            registry = get_registry()\n569\t            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n570\t                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n571\t                if location = entry.display_path\n576\t                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n577\t                else:\n578\t                    logger.info(f\"No resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n582\t            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n583\t            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n584\t            project_cfg = (\n585\t                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n586\t                if classification.project_slug else None\n587\t            )\n588\t\n589\t            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n590\t                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n591\t                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n592\t                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n593\t                context=FetchContext(\n594\t                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n595\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n596\t                    repo_path=repo_path,\n597\t                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n598\t                    host_alias=host_alias,\n599\t                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n600\t                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n601\t                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n602\t                ),\n603\t            )\n604\t\n605\t            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n606\t            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n607\t            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n608\t            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n609\t            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n610\t            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n611\t            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n612\t\n613\t            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n614\t                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n615\t                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n616\t                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n617\t                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n618\t\n619\t                # Increment completed count\n620\t                completed_sources[0] += 1\n621\t\n622\t                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n623\t                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n624\t                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n625\t                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n626\t                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n627\t                    total=total_sources,\n628\t                    source_name=source.value,\n629\t                    source_status=result.status,\n630\t                ))\n631\t\n632\t            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n633\t            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n634\t            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n635\t                timings.record(\n636\t                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n637\t                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n638\t                )\n639\t\n640\t            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n641\t            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n642\t                logger.error(\n643\t                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n644\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n645\t                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n646\t                )\n647\t\n648\t                # Build failed sources list for error event\n649\t                failed_sources = []\n650\t                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n651\t                    failed_sources.append({\n652\t                        \"source\": source.value,\n653\t                        \"status\": result.status,\n654\t                        \"error\": result.error,\n655\t                    })\n656\t\n657\t                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n658\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n659\t                await session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n663\t                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n664\t                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n665\t                )\n666\t\n667\t                # Update intent status to failed\n668\t                store = get_store()\n669\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n670\t                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n671\t                    \"failed\",\n672\t                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n673\t                )\n674\t\n675\t                return {\n676\t                    \"intent_id\": }\n683\t\n684\t            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n685\t            required_sources_failed = [\n686\t                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n687\t                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n688\t                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n689\t            ]\n690\t\n691\t            if project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n716\t                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n717\t                urgency=classification.urgency,\n718\t            )\n719\t\n720\t            synth_start = timings.clock()\n721\t            try:\n722\t                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n723\t            except Exception as synth_e:\n724\t                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n725\t                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n726\t                logger.error(\n727\t                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n728\t                )\n729\t\n730\t                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n731\t                await error_reason=str(synth_e),\n738\t                )\n739\t\n740\t                # Update intent status to failed\n741\t                store = get_store()\n742\t                await \"\"\"\n887\t        # Map enum values by string\n888\t        type_map = {\n889\t            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n890\t            IntentType.ACTION:          IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n897\t        }\n898\t\n899\t        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n900\t        if intent_type ==          return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n905\t            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n906\t                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n907\t\n908\t        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n909\t\n910\t    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n911\t        self,\n912\t        bead_ref: str,\n913\t    ) -> dict | None:\n914\t        \"\"\"\n915\t        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n916\t\n917\t        Args:\n918\t            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n919\t\n920\t      }\n1081\t\n1082\t    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n1083\t        self,\n1084\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n1085\t        timings: DispatchTimings,\n1086\t    ) -> dict:\n1087\t        \"\"\"\n1088\t        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n1089\t\n1090\t        Args:\n1091\t            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n1092\t            timings:               routed_intent.session_id\n1105\t            )\n1106\t\n1107\t            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n1108\t            if degraded-state handling\nclass RouterError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Base exception for router errors.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterTimeoutError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call timed out.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterQuotaError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM call quota exhausted.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterProxyError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router LLM proxy unreachable.\"\"\"\n    pass\n\n\nclass RouterMalformedError(RouterError):\n    \"\"\"Router returned malformed JSON (after corrective retry).\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, parse_error: str, raw_output: str, retry_count: int = 0):\n        self.parse_error = parse_error\n        self.raw_output = raw_output\n        self.retry_count = retry_count\n        super().__init__(f\"Malformed router output (retry {retry_count}): {parse_error}\")\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n    STUCK = \"stuck\"  # Stuck intent - task blocked by circuit breaker\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n    lookup_kind: str | None = None  # lookup intents only: 'logs' | 'config' | 'docs'\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    # router_ms is measured once around classify_utterance() in route_utterance\n    # (one LLM call per utterance) and shared across every intent thread from\n    # that utterance — see Latency Budget & Instrumentation in docs/plan/plan.md.\n    router_ms: int | None = None\n\n\n# Path to the router segmentation prompt. Read from disk on each classify_utterance()\n# call so edits to prompts/router.md take effect without a server restart (hot-reload),\n# matching the pattern in src/synthesize/strand.py (SYNTHESIZE_PROMPT_PATH).\nROUTER_PROMPT_PATH = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/prompts/router.md\")\n\n# Fallback used only if the prompt file cannot be read at runtime.\n_ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK = (\n    \"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp. Segment the utterance into \"\n    \"distinct intent threads, classify each, and return ONLY a JSON array of \"\n    \"intent objects.\"\n)\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None, prompt_path: Optional[Path] = None):\n        self.store = store\n        self.prompt_path = prompt_path or ROUTER_PROMPT_PATH\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create the hot-reload manager (lazy singleton).\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _load_router_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Load the router segmentation prompt from disk (hot-reload, per call).\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.prompt_path.read_text()\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to load router prompt from {self.prompt_path}: {e}\")\n            return _ROUTER_PROMPT_FALLBACK\n\n    def _load_urgency_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load the urgency classification prompt from disk via the hot-reload manager.\n\n        prompts/urgency.md is a separately hot-reloadable artifact (registered in\n        src/components/hot_reload.py). Splicing it here keeps urgency guidance\n        editable independently of the segmentation prompt. Returns \"\" on failure\n        so the router still functions without urgency guidance.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self._get_reload_manager().get_prompt(\"urgency\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to load urgency prompt: {e}\")\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _build_system_prompt(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build the full system prompt: segmentation prompt + urgency rules.\"\"\"\n        system_prompt = self._load_router_prompt()\n        urgency_prompt = self._load_urgency_prompt()\n        if urgency_prompt:\n            system_prompt = (\n                f\"{system_prompt}\\n\\n## Urgency Classification Rules\\n\\n{urgency_prompt}\"\n            )\n        return system_prompt\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        retry_on_malformed: bool = True,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n            retry_on_malformed: If True, perform one corrective retry on JSON parse failure\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterTimeoutError: LLM call timed out\n            RouterQuotaError: LLM quota exhausted\n            RouterProxyError: LLM proxy unreachable\n            RouterMalformedError: Malformed JSON after corrective retry\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        # Track retry attempt for malformed JSON\n        retry_count = 0\n        raw_response = None\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Store raw response for error reporting\n            raw_response = response\n\n            # Parse JSON response using centralized parser (optimized with manual fence stripping)\n            # Let ParseLLMError bubble to retry handler below\n            intents_data = parse_llm_response(response)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                    lookup_kind=intent_data.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP else None,\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except ParseLLMError as e:\n            \"\"\"\n            First parsing attempt failed - implement corrective retry.\n\n            Router uses corrective retry because it's the first step in the pipeline.\n            A malformed router response cascades to everything downstream.\n            One retry is justified because it's cheap (same prompt, 2048 tokens).\n            \"\"\"\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response: {e}\")\n            raw_output = e.raw_response or raw_response  # Preserve for error reporting\n\n            # Implement one corrective retry on malformed JSON\n            if retry_on_malformed and retry_count == 0:\n                logger.info(\"Malformed JSON detected, attempting corrective retry...\")\n                retry_count += 1\n                try:\n                    # Retry once with same parameters\n                    return await self.classify_utterance(\n                        utterance=utterance,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        retry_on_malformed=False,  # Prevent infinite retry\n                    )\n                except ParseLLMError as retry_e:\n                    # Retry also failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n                    raise RouterMalformedError(\n                        parse_error=str(retry_e),\n                        raw_output=retry_e.raw_response or raw_response,\n                        retry_count=retry_count,\n                    ) from retry_e\n\n            # No retry allowed or retry failed - raise RouterMalformedError\n            raise RouterMalformedError(\n                parse_error=str(e),\n                raw_output=raw_output,\n                retry_count=retry_count,\n            ) from e\n\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, asyncio.TimeoutError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\")\n            raise RouterTimeoutError(f\"Router LLM call timed out: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMRateLimitError,) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            raise RouterQuotaError(f\"Router LLM call quota exhausted: {e}\") from e\n\n        except (LLMError, httpx.HTTPError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\")\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM proxy unreachable: {e}\") from e\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed with unexpected error: {e}\")\n            # Wrap unknown errors as RouterProxyError for graceful degradation\n            raise RouterProxyError(f\"Router LLM call failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n\n        Raises:\n            RouterError: If router fails and broadcasts error event\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate a fallback intent_id for error reporting\n        error_intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        try:\n            # Classify the utterance (one LLM call per utterance — the router stage\n            # is shared across every intent thread it produces, so its duration is\n            # measured once here and stamped onto each RoutedIntent).\n            classify_start = time.monotonic()\n            classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n            router_ms = int((time.monotonic() - classify_start) * 1000)\n\n            routed_intents = []\n            for classification in classifications:\n                # Create intent ID\n                intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n                routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                    intent_id=intent_id,\n                    classification=classification,\n                    session_id=session_id,\n                    utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n                    router_ms=router_ms,\n                )\n                routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n            return routed_intents\n\n        except RouterMalformedError as e:\n            # Malformed JSON after corrective retry - broadcast clarification card\n            logger.error(f\"Router malformed after retry: {e.parse_error}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_clarification_card(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                parse_error=e.parse_error,\n                retry_count=e.retry_count,\n                raw_output_snippet=e.raw_output[:200] if e.raw_output else None,\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterTimeoutError as e:\n            # Router LLM timeout - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router timeout: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"timeout\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterQuotaError as e:\n            # Router quota exhausted - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router quota exhausted: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"quota_exhausted\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n        except RouterProxyError as e:\n            # Router proxy unreachable - broadcast router_unavailable\n            logger.error(f\"Router proxy error: {e}\")\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_router_unavailable(\n                utterance=utterance,\n                intent_id=error_intent_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                error_reason=\"proxy_down\",\n            )\n            raise  # Re-raise so caller knows routing failed\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Capture per-stage timings for this dispatch and persist one\n        # dispatch_timings row (see Latency Budget & Instrumentation). Each\n        # branch records the stages it measures; router_ms is shared across\n        # the utterance's threads and was stamped on the RoutedIntent. Capture\n        # is non-fatal: a persistence failure logs and moves on, never breaking\n        # the dispatch itself.\n        timings = DispatchTimings()\n        timings.record(\"router_ms\", routed_intent.router_ms)\n\n        # Honesty guards for unimplemented intents\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.ACTION:\n            # Action executor is NOT BUILT — broadcast design-only card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_action_design_only(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect the design-only state\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Action execution is design-only — executor not built\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"design_only\",\n                \"message\": \"Action execution is not yet available\",\n            }\n\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER:\n            # Reminders are NOT YET IMPLEMENTED — broadcast clarification card\n            handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n            await handler.broadcast_reminder_unavailable(\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Update intent status to reflect that reminders are unavailable\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"resolved\",\n                \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"unavailable\",\n                \"message\": \"Reminders are not available yet\",\n            }\n\n        try:\n            # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n            if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n                result = await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent, timings)\n            else:\n                # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n                result = await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent, timings)\n        except Exception:\n            # Persist whatever was captured before the failure, then re-raise.\n            await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n            raise\n\n        await self._persist_timings(routed_intent.intent_id, timings)\n        return result\n\n    async def _persist_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist the captured dispatch timings. Non-fatal on error.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.record_dispatch_timings(intent_id, **timings.to_fields())\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"dispatch timings not recorded for {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records fetch_first_source_ms,\n                fetch_total_ms, and synthesize_total_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(\n                classification.intent_type,\n                classification.lookup_kind\n            )\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            from ..registry import get_project\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            # YAML registry entry carries cluster/namespace/argocd_app — cluster\n            # drives ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh: the fetch strand\n            # resolves {argocd_api} from `cluster` via config/clusters.yaml).\n            # argocd_app defaults to the slug when omitted (see _fetch_argocd_app).\n            project_cfg = (\n                get_project(classification.project_slug)\n                if classification.project_slug else None\n            )\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                    cluster=project_cfg.get(\"cluster\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    namespace=project_cfg.get(\"namespace\") if project_cfg else None,\n                    app_name=project_cfg.get(\"argocd_app\") if project_cfg else None,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            # fetch_first_source_ms = time from fetch start to the first source\n            # resolving (success/fail/timeout — the first progress state on the\n            # pending card). fetch_total_ms = the fetch window close.\n            fetch_start = timings.clock()\n            first_source_at: list[float | None] = [None]\n            total_sources = len(get_fetch_commands(fetch_intent_type))\n            completed_sources: list[int] = [0]\n\n            def _on_fetch_progress(source, result) -> None:\n                \"\"\"Track fetch timing and broadcast progress events to pending card.\"\"\"\n                # Track first source timing for fetch_first_source_ms metric\n                if first_source_at[0] is None:\n                    first_source_at[0] = timings.clock()\n\n                # Increment completed count\n                completed_sources[0] += 1\n\n                # Broadcast progress event to canvas ('3/5 sources in')\n                asyncio.create_task(broadcast_fetch_progress(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    completed=completed_sources[0],\n                    total=total_sources,\n                    source_name=source.value,\n                    source_status=result.status,\n                ))\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request, _on_fetch_progress)\n            timings.record(\"fetch_total_ms\", fetch_result.total_duration_ms)\n            if first_source_at[0] is not None:\n                timings.record(\n                    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n                    timings.elapsed_ms(fetch_start, first_source_at[0]),\n                )\n\n            # Check for terminal failure: ALL sources failed (Degraded-State UX)\n            if fetch_result.terminal_failure == \"all_sources_failed\":\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.failed)} failed, \"\n                    f\"{len(fetch_result.coverage.timed_out)} timed out\"\n                )\n\n                # Build failed sources list for error event\n                failed_sources = []\n                for source, result in fetch_result.sources.items():\n                    failed_sources.append({\n                        \"source\": source.value,\n                        \"status\": result.status,\n                        \"error\": result.error,\n                    })\n\n                # Broadcast all_sources_failed error event\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_all_sources_failed(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    failed_sources=failed_sources,\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    \"All fetch sources failed\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"No data — all required sources failed\",\n                    \"terminal_failure\": \"all_sources_failed\",\n                }\n\n            # Check for required source failures (terminal condition)\n            required_sources_failed = [\n                s for s in fetch_result.coverage.failed\n                if any(cmd.source == s and cmd.required\n                       for cmd in get_fetch_commands(fetch_result.intent_type))\n            ]\n\n            if required_sources_failed:\n                failure_reason = f\"Required data sources failed: {', '.join(s.value for s in required_sources_failed)}\"\n                logger.error(f\"Required sources failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n                # Handle terminal failure\n                await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                    routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                    failure_reason=failure_reason,\n                    error_type=\"required_source_failure\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": failure_reason,\n                    \"message\": \"Required sources failed - cannot proceed\",\n                }\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synth_start = timings.clock()\n            try:\n                synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n            except Exception as synth_e:\n                # ZAI failure at synthesize stage - broadcast degraded_raw_data\n                # (Degraded-State UX: fetched data is never discarded)\n                logger.error(\n                    f\"Synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {synth_e}\"\n                )\n\n                handler = get_degraded_state_handler()\n                await handler.broadcast_degraded_raw_data(\n                    intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                    fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                    error_reason=str(synth_e),\n                )\n\n                # Update intent status to failed\n                store = get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"failed\",\n                    f\"Synthesize failed: {synth_e}\",\n                )\n\n                return {\n                    \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                    \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                    \"status\": \"failed\",\n                    \"error\": \"synthesize_failed\",\n                    \"message\": \"Summary unavailable — showing raw fetch data\",\n                    \"degraded\": True,\n                }\n\n            timings.record(\"synthesize_total_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(synth_start))\n            # synthesize_first_token_ms is not measurable on the current\n            # call_simple path (no token stream) and is left NULL until the\n            # synthesize strand streams — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent classification (includes lookup_kind for lookups)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=synthesize_result.data,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag so client knows which path to take\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"card_fallback\": render_outcome.card_fallback,\n                \"rendered_html\": render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                \"component_id\": render_outcome.component_id,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Detect terminal failure and handle appropriately\n            await self._handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n                routed_intent=routed_intent,\n                failure_reason=str(e),\n                error_type=\"worker_crash\",\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Fetch/synthesize failed - terminal error\",\n            }\n\n    async def _handle_terminal_failure_for_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        failure_reason: str,\n        error_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle terminal failure for a routed intent.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The intent that failed\n            failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n            error_type: Type of error (worker_crash, invalid_input, required_source_failure)\n            bead_ref: Associated bead reference (if applicable)\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..escalate.handler import handle_terminal_failure\n\n        # Get or create topic for failed card\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Failed: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                topic_type=\"exception\",\n            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n            topic_id = None\n\n        # Handle terminal failure (updates intent status, stores in bead_watch, broadcasts SSE)\n        await handle_terminal_failure(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            failure_reason=failure_reason,\n            error_type=error_type,\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n        )\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType, lookup_kind: str | None = None) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\n\n        For lookup intents, route to the subtype-specific fetch matrix based on lookup_kind.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n            IntentType.STUCK: FetchIntentType.STUCK,\n        }\n\n        # For lookup intents with lookup_kind, route to the subtype-specific matrix\n        if intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP and lookup_kind:\n            if lookup_kind == \"logs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_LOGS\n            elif lookup_kind == \"config\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_CONFIG\n            elif lookup_kind == \"docs\":\n                return FetchIntentType.LOOKUP_DOCS\n\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _check_fence_for_bead(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n    ) -> dict | None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a bead has been fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at set).\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead reference to check\n\n        Returns:\n            Fence context dict with bead_id, refusal_reason, refusal_count, fenced_at\n            if fenced, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            bead_watch = await store.get_bead_watch(bead_ref)\n\n            if not bead_watch:\n                return None\n\n            # Check if bead is fenced (has last_refusal_reason or fenced_at)\n            last_refusal_reason = bead_watch.get(\"last_refusal_reason\")\n            fenced_at = bead_watch.get(\"fenced_at\")\n            refusal_count = bead_watch.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n            if last_refusal_reason or fenced_at:\n                # Bead is fenced - extract fence context\n                return {\n                    \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                    \"refusal_reason\": last_refusal_reason or \"Fenced (no reason provided)\",\n                    \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                    \"fenced_at\": fenced_at,\n                }\n\n            return None\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking fence status for bead {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n            return None\n\n    async def _create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        fence_context: dict,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a stuck card when a fenced bead is detected during intent routing.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent\n            fence_context: Fence context from _check_fence_for_bead\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with stuck card details\n        \"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime\n\n        bead_id = fence_context[\"bead_id\"]\n        refusal_reason = fence_context[\"refusal_reason\"]\n        refusal_count = fence_context[\"refusal_count\"]\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Creating stuck card for fenced bead {bead_id} \"\n            f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n\n            # Update intent type to 'stuck' and status to 'stuck'\n            await store.update_intent_type_and_status(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=\"stuck\",\n                status=\"stuck\",\n            )\n\n            # Get or create topic for this stuck card\n            topic_type = \"project\" if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else \"research\"\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=f\"Fenced: {routed_intent.utterance[:80]}\",\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[routed_intent.classification.project_slug] if routed_intent.classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n\n            # Link intent to topic\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n\n            # Create stuck result\n            summary = f\"Task stuck — needs your input\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": f\"This task has been blocked after {refusal_count} refusals.\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"Review the bead and provide the missing information or context needed to proceed.\",\n                \"fence_detected_during\": \"intent_routing\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from original intent classification (not \"stuck\" state, includes lookup_kind)\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=routed_intent.classification.intent_type.value,\n                project_slug=routed_intent.classification.project_slug,\n                lookup_kind=routed_intent.classification.lookup_kind,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            # Render stuck card via hot-path selector (deterministic, no LLM)\n            renderer = get_renderer()\n            render_outcome = renderer.render(\n                result_id=result_id,\n                result_type=result_type,\n                result_data=data,\n                summary=summary,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n            )\n\n            # Update result's card_fallback flag\n            await store.update_result_card_fallback(result_id, render_outcome.card_fallback)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created stuck card {result_id} for fenced bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            # Broadcast task_stuck event via SSE\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.TASK_STUCK,\n                    data={\n                        \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                        \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                        \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                        \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": routed_intent.session_id,\n                        \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                        \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"stuck\",\n                \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"stuck_reason\": refusal_reason,\n                \"refusal_count\": refusal_count,\n                \"message\": \"Fenced bead detected during intent routing\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create stuck card from fence: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": \"stuck\",\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to create stuck card from fence context\",\n            }\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n        timings: DispatchTimings,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLESS bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n            timings: Per-stage timing collector; records escalate_ms.\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card or stuck card if fence detected\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Step 1: Check for existing fenced beads in the session before escalating\n        # This detects fence events when last_refusal_reason is set on existing beads\n        try:\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            fenced_beads = await store.get_fenced_beads_for_session(\n                routed_intent.session_id\n            )\n\n            # If there are fenced beads, extract fence context and create stuck card\n            if fenced_beads:\n                # Get the most recently fenced bead\n                most_recent_fenced = fenced_beads[0]\n                bead_ref = most_recent_fenced[\"bead_ref\"]\n                refusal_reason = most_recent_fenced.get(\"last_refusal_reason\", \"Fenced (no reason provided)\")\n                refusal_count = most_recent_fenced.get(\"refusal_count\", 0)\n\n \"fenced_at\": most_recent_fenced.get(\"fenced_at\"),\n                }\n\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Detected fenced bead {bead_ref} for session {routed_intent.session_id} \"\n                    f\"(intent {routed_intent.intent_id})\"\n                )\n\n                # Create stuck card with fence context\n                return await self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                    routed_intent, fence_context\n                )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Error checking for fenced beads: {e}\")\n            # Continue to escalation if fence check fails\n\n        # Step 2: No fenced beads detected - proceed with escalation\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead — escalate_ms budgets formulation + validation +\n            # bf create (task-profile dispatches only; NULL for hot-path rows).\n            esc_start = timings.clock()\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n            timings.record(\"escalate_ms\", timings.elapsed_ms(esc_start))\n\n            # Check if the created bead is immediately fenced (race condition:\n            # watcher may have fenced it between escalate request and now)\n            if result.bead_id:\n                fence_context = self._create_stuck_card_from_fence(\n                        routed_intent, fence_context\n                    )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"escalated\",\n                \"bead_id\": result.bead_id,\n                \"pending_card\": {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_febd827ccc934dd2a571e2ce","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# aide-de-camp: Application Plan\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\taide-de-camp is a universal personal interface that accepts stream-of-consciousness voice and text input, routes it to parallel agents across any domain, and renders results as a live canvas of HTML cards or as synthesized audio narration. It is built on top of existing personal infrastructure and improves itself through conversational feedback after initial bootstrap.\n6\t\n7\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n8\t\n9\t---\n10\t\n11\t## Problem Statement\n12\t\n13\tManaging multiple active projects requires constant context-switching between dedicated tabs, terminals, and chat sessions. The input channel (typing into a specific Claude Code tab) is also the routing layer, and that conflation adds cognitive overhead.
